stem: A education study measures average test score improvement across 4 schools, each containing multiple classrooms. school A has 20 observations with mean 53.8 points (within-school SD = 3.3). school B has 8 observations with mean 40.2 points (within-school SD = 9.0). school C has 15 observations with mean 41.5 points (within-school SD = 5.5). school D has 15 observations with mean 41.1 points (within-school SD = 5.0). The between-school standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 5.8 and 14.5 points. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true average test score improvement for school D, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-school variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for school D: 41.2 points (shrunk from raw 41.1). Range [41.1, 41.3] as between-school SD varies from 5.8 to 14.5. Pooled mean: 45.5.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 45.5 points (weighted average of 4 school means).
Shrinkage factor for school D: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/15). At σ_between=9.7: B = 0.983.
Interval [41.1, 41.3] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (41.1) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (45.5) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
